publications.name: How does Multi-Task Training Affect Transformer In-Context Capabilities? Investigations with Function Classes
publications.publisher: In Proceedings of the 2024 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics 2024
publications.summary: Large language models (LLM) have recently shown the extraordinary ability to perform unseen tasks based on few-shot examples provided as text, also known as in-context learning (ICL). While recent works have attempted to understand the mechanisms driving ICL, few have explored training strategies that incentivize these models to generalize to multiple tasks. Multi-task learning (MTL) for generalist models is a promising direction that offers transfer learning potential, enabling large parameterized models to be trained from simpler, related tasks. In this work, we investigate the combination of MTL with ICL to build models that efficiently learn tasks while being robust to out-of-distribution examples. We propose several effective curriculum learning strategies that allow ICL models to achieve higher data efficiency and more stable convergence. Our experiments reveal that ICL models can effectively learn difficult tasks by training on progressively harder tasks while mixing in prior tasks, denoted as mixed curriculum in this work.

awards.title: Sophomore Research Fellowship
awards.summary:
•	Collaborate with my research professor from Undergraduate Research Scholars in creating and conducting a research project based around Natural Language Processing in the field of psychology
•	Selected as 1 of 27 students to receive this grant out of all colleges in the University of Wisconsin-Madison
•	Dedicate approximately 225 hours to this project with the expectation of presenting findings in the Spring of 2023

awards.title: Fiscal Challenge - 3rd Place
awards.summary:
• Assisted in the planning, research, and creation of the presentation for the 2022 Fiscal Challenge as one of six presentation members
• Developed a budget plan in which the debt-to-GDP ratio was 83% by Q2 2051, 15% under what was expected of us
• Proposed a solution through a 20 minute presentation with elaborate graphs and statistics
• One of six teams that traveled to Washington, DC and defended our plans in front of a live panel of judges

awards.title: College Fed Challenge - Top 6
awards.summary:
• Assisted in the planning, research, and creation of the presentation for the 2021 College Fed Challenge as an alternate for the presentation team
• Analyzed the current economic conditions for about 6 months through research papers and articles
• Proposed a solution through a 15 minute presentation with elaborate graphs and statistics

awards.title: Department of Economics, Case Competition - 1st Place
awards.summary:
• Created a solution to improve overdraft policies for credit unions, while preserving member satisfaction and keeping revenue up
• Referenced papers from other universities to devise a policy
• Delivered a 8-10 minute presentation with four other members

awards.title: 2021 Township of Palatine Service Award
awards.summary:
• TOPS awards are given to people in the community who have done outstanding community service
• I received this award due to my Eagle Project which consisted of making budget computers for underprivileged families in my community

awards.title: Eagle Scout
awards.summary:
• Conceptualized personal community service project, fundraised over $1k from local businesses, coordinated logistics with my local township, and lead 5 volunteers in the building of 15 PCs that came with manuals using Raspberry Pis
• Donated PCs to underprivileged families through my local township
• Presented my Eagle project at the local township meetings with PowerPoint and speeches
